heading: Certifying checks on State banks admitted as members
label: 331 Certifying checks on State banks admitted as members
text: It shall be unlawful for any officer, clerk, or agent of any bank admitted to membership under authority of this subchapter, to certify any check drawn upon such bank unless the person or company drawing the check has on deposit therewith at the time such check is certified an amount of money equal to the amount specified in such check. Any check so certified by duly authorized officers shall be a good and valid obligation against such bank, but the act of any such officer, clerk, or agent in violation of this subchapter, may subject such bank to a forfeiture of its membership in the Federal reserve system upon hearing by the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.
notes: References in TextThis subchapter, referred to in text, was in the original “this section”, meaning section 9 of act , which is classified generally to this subchapter (§ 321 et seq.).Dec. 23, 1913

CodificationSection is comprised of the fourteenth par. of section 9 of act , as amended. The fourteenth par. constituted the eleventh par. of section 9 in 1917 (), became the twelfth par. in 1927 (), became the thirteenth par. in 1935 (), and became the fourteenth par. in 1950 (). For further details, see Codification notes set out under sections 321 and 329a of this title.Dec. 23, 191340 Stat. 23244 Stat. 122949 Stat. 70464 Stat. 458
Change of NameSection 203(a) of act , changed name of Federal Reserve Board to Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.Aug. 23, 1935
